_doc: Fact-driven cinematic stories. Each beat names a DIFFERENT mechanic that fits the fact — never the same one twice in a video, and no two videos built the same. The brain authors these; a validator enforces variety.

stories.title: How Big Is a Blue Whale, Really?
stories.narration: How big is a blue whale, really? It's as long as three school buses, about ninety feet. Its tongue alone weighs five thousand pounds, as much as a full grown elephant. All together, two hundred tons, the largest animal that has ever lived.

stories.title: How Much of Earth Is Actually Water?
stories.narration: How much of Earth is water? Water covers seventy one percent of the surface. But drinkable fresh water? Only three percent. The other ninety seven percent is salt. And the water we can actually reach is barely half a percent.

stories.title: Just How Tall Is Mount Everest?

beats.top: At the summit the air holds
beats.subject: oxygen mask climber
beats.compare: of the oxygen at sea level

stories.narration: Just how tall is Mount Everest? Stack ten Burj Khalifas and you're close, twenty nine thousand feet up. At the summit the air holds only a third of the oxygen you breathe at sea level. And it's seventy six below, cold enough to freeze skin in minutes.
